data.body:
G4 re-confirmation (parent 3fe1 + this reconciler-sweep child, whole roster considered). Independently re-verified the two prior cross-child findings against source; both hold high-confidence.

Verified facts:
- `rebar_reconciler` is a TOP-LEVEL package put on PYTHONPATH for subprocess use only (_engine.py:75-96, `python -m rebar_reconciler`), so its ~16 `logging.getLogger(__name__)` loggers resolve under `rebar_reconciler.*`, NOT the `rebar` root.
- `rebar_reconciler/__init__.py` is a single comment line — no NullHandler.
- No `StreamHandler` exists anywhere under `src/rebar` today (0 hits) — matches the audit's "no handler configured anywhere"; substrate child 8fbd only installs handlers at CLI + MCP mains and a NullHandler on the `rebar` ROOT, leaving the reconciler subprocess + `rebar_reconciler.*` namespace unwired.
- Probes capture `python -m rebar_reconciler "$@" 2>&1` (e2e_validation_probe.sh:75, e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:135) and grep `^(FILTERED|filter:|OK:|ERROR:)` (17 sites), consuming the `ERROR:`-prefixed stderr prints (__main__.py:413-416, documented at :398-404).

FINDING 1 — Dependency gap + conflicting assumptions: this child's AC "migrate stderr-diagnostic prints (reconcile.py, __main__.py) to the named logger" cannot coexist with parent SC2 "observable on a stderr logger", because no sibling stands up a handler for the reconciler's third subprocess entrypoint or bridges `rebar_reconciler.*` to the `rebar` root. Migrated INFO is dropped; WARNING+ falls to Python's last-resort handler in a different format. Cross-checked whole roster: no sibling installs a handler at `rebar_reconciler/__main__.main()`.
  suggested_fix: re-scope 8fbd (or this child) to install a stderr StreamHandler at `rebar_reconciler/__main__.py:main()` and/or bridge `rebar_reconciler` to `rebar`; add an AC asserting reconciler-subprocess diagnostics land on stderr; add cross-dep on 8fbd. Else strike the print->logger migration bullet to remove the contradiction.

FINDING 2 — Consumer impact / residual references: migrating the `ERROR:`-prefixed stderr prints to `logger.error(...)` breaks the `^ERROR:` grep contract the jira-pressure-test probes depend on (no formatter emits an `ERROR:` token, and per Finding 1 no handler/formatter is configured for this subprocess). A consumer silently loses its failure signal — the stderr-side analogue of the parent's stdout-is-sacred constraint.
  suggested_fix: preserve the `ERROR:` token via a configured formatter on the reconciler handler, OR carve out the probe-contract `ERROR:`-prefixed stderr prints as data-like output that stays `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` (parallel to the CLI stdout-data carve-out). Add an AC asserting the probe `ERROR:`-grep contract is preserved.

Modes clean for this pairing: implicit shared state (_errors.py helpers shared read-only, characterization-tested first); scope overlap (reconciler package uniquely owned — no other sweep touches _engine/rebar_reconciler/); ordering violation (LATE/last + blocks 0ec1 + downstream of 16a1 is consistent).
